title: baccaurea dasystachya
intro: Baccaurea dasystachya, also known as the Malay ash tree, is a species of plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Baccaurea dasystachya include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections, as well as being used as a treatment for gastrointestinal issues and wounds. Herbal preparations of Baccaurea dasystachya include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
